2605.05773.title: CircuitFormer: A Circuit Language Model for Analog Topology Design from Natural Language Prompt
2605.05773.paper_summary: Automating analog circuit design remains a longstanding challenge in Electronic Design Automation (EDA). While Transformer-based Large Language Models (LLMs) have revolutionized software code generation, their application to analog hardware design is hindered by two critical limitations: (i) the scarcity of analog design datasets containing natural language description of a design and its corresponding netlist, and (ii) the inefficiency of general-purpose tokenizers (e.g., Byte Pair Encoding (BPE)) in capturing the inherent graph structure of circuits. To bridge this gap, first, we curate the largest annotated dataset of analog circuit netlists to date, comprising 31,341 netlist-natural language description pairs across all major circuit classes. Furthermore, we propose Circuit Tokenizer (CKT), a novel circuit graph tokenizer designed to encode netlist connectivity by explicitly mining frequent subcircuits. In terms of scalability, CKT overcomes the bottleneck of prior circuit graph serialization methods where vocabulary size scales linearly with maximum number of components in the dataset, n_max, (O(n_max)); instead, CKT decouples vocabulary growth from circuit complexity, achieving a constant O(1) complexity. Empirically, CKT outperforms standard BPE on circuit topology representation, reducing sequence length by 57% and achieving a 2.3x superior compression ratio using a compact, fixed vocabulary of size 512. Leveraging this optimized tokenization, we train a circuit-specific language model, CircuitFormer, a 511M parameter encoder-decoder transformer. Our model achieves 100% syntactic correctness and an 83% functional success rate across all major analog circuit categories, outperforming state-of-the-art open-source LLMs by 10% and 14%, respectively, while requiring 240x fewer parameters. The dataset is publicly available at https://huggingface.co/datasets/touhid314/cktformer-dataset.
2605.05773.paper_summary_zh: 自動化類比電路設計仍然是電子設計自動化（EDA）中的一項長期挑戰。雖然基於Transformer的大型語言模型（LLMs）已經徹底改變了軟體代碼生成，但它們在類比硬體設計中的應用受到兩個關鍵限制的阻礙：（i）缺乏包含設計自然語言描述及其對應網路清單的類比設計數據集，以及（ii）通用分詞器（例如，字節對編碼（BPE））在捕捉電路固有圖結構方面的低效。為了填補這一空白，我們首先整理了迄今為止最大的類比電路網路清單註釋數據集，涵蓋了所有主要電路類別中的31,341對網路清單-自然語言描述。此外，我們提出了Circuit Tokenizer（CKT），這是一種新穎的電路圖分詞器，旨在通過明確挖掘頻繁的子電路來編碼網路清單的連通性。在可擴展性方面，CKT克服了先前電路圖序列化方法的瓶頸，這些方法的詞彙大小隨著數據集中最大元件數量n_max（O(n_max)）線性增長；相反，CKT將詞彙增長與電路複雜性解耦，實現了恆定的O(1)複雜度。在實證上，CKT在電路拓撲表示上超越了標準BPE，將序列長度減少了57%，並使用大小為512的緊湊固定詞彙達到了2.3倍的優越壓縮比。利用這種優化的分詞，我們訓練了一個電路特定的語言模型CircuitFormer，一個511M參數的編碼器-解碼器Transformer。我們的模型在所有主要類比電路類別中達到了100%的語法正確性和83%的功能成功率，分別超越了最先進的開源LLMs 10%和14%，同時需要的參數少了240倍。該數據集可在https://huggingface.co/datasets/touhid314/cktformer-dataset公開獲取。
2605.05773.author: Md Touhidul Islam et.al.
2605.05773.authors: Md Touhidul Islam, Sujan Kumar Saha, Farimah Farahmandi, Mark Tehranipoor
